headline: Technical Debt
description: Technical debt (Ward Cunningham, 1992) is the implied cost of future rework caused by choosing an expedient solution now instead of a better approach that would take longer. Like financial debt, it accrues interest — the longer you delay, the more it costs to fix.

citation.name: Managing Technical Debt: A Practical Guide (2025 Edition)
citation.author: multiple

citation.name: Technical Debt: A 2025 Systematic Mapping Study
citation.author: multiple
